tokens.text: Thirst for Power
The Legacy of Ethric the Mad grants its bearer unimaginable power. Several contenders are ready to fight for the right to claim it.
Thirst for Power
Although immense, the legacy of Ethric the Mad may belong to only one seat of power.
Eliminate all competitors
Devir must survive
Maelstrom must survive
Tss’kish must survive
Triumvirate’s Order
Meet the informant at the University
Capture Ethric’s Gold mine
Capture Ethric’s city
Rise to Grace
Visit the Infernal Cirque
Defeat the Infernal Cirque army
Reach the Eternal Dragon
Whispers of Trouble
Meet with the Tree of Knowledge
Visit Ethric’s Lost Library
Devir, Son of Devir
Maelstrom
Elder Tss’kish
Gleard the Grey
Minotaur
Reaver
Zoran, the Ringmaster
Tree of Knowledge
Hoplet

“To Devir… If you are reading this message, we trust you have reached your destination and stand ready to begin your charge. Yet recent reports suggest new complications we deem you must be warned of.”
Hrm… This already smells of trouble.

“Though your task remains unchanged — to tame these lands and uncover the source of Ethric’s vast wealth — other forces have set their gaze upon the wizard’s legacy. Their armies gather even now. Expect to cross paths both with flaming and fae troops, and know that conflict may prove unavoidable.”
“Proceed as planned, but account for these rivals. See that they do not hinder your purpose. Your contact awaits you at the nearby university and will provide further knowledge.”
“Alvar Diruil. For the glory of Alvar.”
Signed, Caspair Silvertongue… Hmph. So much for an easy march.
Is Devir, son of Devir, grown soft at the sight of challenge?
Soft? Never. A path with fangs is still a path forward. Now gather your gear, brothers. We march.
The task is plain enough: cleanse these mountains of cave‑crawl and forest‑scuttle that plague them. A dull chore, were it not for the pleasant screams it promises.
So, what say you to a brief detour at the Infernal Cirque? A little merriment ere we get to work?
A churning swarm of reavers erupts in shrill, wicked laughter. Their claw‑blades clatter like excited teeth, eager for the slaughter awaiting them upon the Cirque’s blood‑slick arena.
I shall take it as a resounding “aye”! Come, then — let us make haste!
Young one… must you tangle yourself in my branches…? Your fluttering makes my sap stir all the wrong ways.
What else are we to do on the trail? Travelling on your root‑feet is slower than a seed waiting for thaw beneath a winter crust!
Slow or not, we have arrived all the same.
Woosh! Truly? Then let’s buzz on with the task!

That is what I have been trying to do…  I must reach for the deep memory‑root that holds what my bark alone cannot. Even the oldest oak cannot carry the whole forest within itself, but roots always remember.
How did a slow old tree like you ever go wandering that much?

You know only the crowns of trees, young sprout. But below the soil, we reach far… and we touch one another. What we learn is shared. What we forget is held for us. And when we return, the memories come back changed — weathered by time.
Among these woods, I sense one I once called friend. An ancient one, older even than I. He will remember more, and show us where this quiet land hides its secrets.
Then let’s not dawdle. I don’t like sneaky knife‑ear walkers and fiery multipede crawlers trampling the grass nearby.
Are you the informant I was sent to find?
That would be me. Devir of Alvar, I assume? I trust your journey was not hindered?
Not of Alvar, I am from Balthazar’s Lair. Nevertheless, I stand before you, and that is all that matters. Tell me what you’ve learned about the old wizard and his hoarded wealth.
Quite a lot, in truth. Ethric the Mad chose this forsaken corner of the land not merely for its solitude… but for the mountain that shelters a vein of gold rich enough to fund all his workings.
And scarcely touched it. Which means there may be far greater riches waiting still beneath the stone. I’ve marked the site here.
Claim it, and the Triumvirate will surely consider your charge fulfilled.
Claiming a mine alone will not see the task done.
How so?
Any unguarded treasure draws thieves like flies. Without proper watch, workers fall to greed and schemes. And rival powers will descend the moment word spreads.
We need a stronghold — a place to raise arms, post guards, and house Alvar’s trusted stewards. Do you know of such a site?
Let me see… yes… here.
Ethric once held this town as his seat. It lies close by, and if taken, it could serve well.
Good. It will do.
Then I await tidings of your victory. For Alvar!
For Alvar.
Both the mine and the town lie under our banner. Time to report our success to the Triumvirate.
About that… I believe they may already know. Another letter arrived from Alvar.
I doubt that. We have scarcely finished securing the place. Hand it here.
“To Devir… By Caspair’s calculations — and expectations — you might have finished securing the source of Ethric’s wealth by now.”
Expectations, certainly. Calculations? I doubt it.

“With this letter comes an artifact to aid you in your next charge. You and your host are to remain in the mountains and bring order to the region, ensuring that the gold begins its rightful flow toward our mighty capital.”

“I know the bustle of Alvar may not suit your temperament, yet we deem you well‑fitted for stewardship of a more remote domain. Consider these lands yours for the present. Fund shall be provided from the capital.”
Huh.
“And should Silvertongue’s expectations of you remain unmet, I advise you to see them fulfilled swiftly. As you know, his patience is… limited.”
“Alvar Diruil.” Signed, Balthazar, Son of Balthazar.
Did I get it right, am I some kind of a landlord now?..
Sounds like you’ve been promoted.
That is one way to put it.
We all know you have little love for the bustle and hustle of Alvar — the great capital of elves. You should rejoice!
Alvar is our capital as well. But you are right. Let us look at the bright side.
[Morale increased by 3. You gained the Holy Sigil of Roph artifact!]

The road bends, and soon the grotesque splendour of the Infernal Cirque rises before you. The air trembles with roaring flame, clashing steel, and the thunderous cheer of an audience hungry for ruin. Once, no mortal would have dreamt that such hellish festivities could stand openly upon Jadame’s soil… yet here they flourish, bright as a wound.
Well then… shall we indulge ourselves?
Step right in! Step right in! The Cirque is ever eager for fresh performers!
Though — hahahahaha! — they seldom remain fresh for long!
Do your worst. I swear upon the Lord of the Fly’s name: I shall not fall a second time! [Attack]
Another moment, host. I have preparations yet to make. [Leave]
Have you gathered your courage, performer? Are you ready to step beneath the flame?
No time to waste! Press on. [Attack]
Fall back for now. [Leave]
Applause! Applause! What a spectacle! Such lovely lethality, such spirited carnage — look at them! The crowds are beside themselves with delight!
And what of you, ringmaster? Are you bold enough to face a challenge of your own?
Ha! Not this day, my ravaging friend. I value my limbs entirely too much. But I do have something for you, a reward most fitting.
Go on.
I know full well why you and your host stalk these lands, so lend me your ear. A sliver of knowledge may serve you better than any trinket I could thrust into your hand.
Information only? That is the reward you boast of?

Now, now — no need to bristle before hearing the tale! You would not believe the creatures that wander through my tent‑flaps, and the whispers they bring. The word among them is this: after some mad wizard conducted his even madder experiments in these mountains, the very soil grew fat with the death magic… so saturated, in fact, that a clutch of dragon bones clawed their way back to unlife.
So what I hear is: a lich dragon prowls the wilds, eager to join the Chorus and help set our foes ablaze. That suits me splendidly.
A fair warning, though. This rumour came from a coven of vampires passing through. Should you cross paths with the dragon, expect them to be lurking nearby as well.
[The Lich Dragon joined your army]
So… you and I both have found our own ways to defy death, have we not?
Come then. Let us finish our work here and return to the King.
[Morale increased by 3. You gained the Demon Claw artifact!]
Ahh, an adventurer! I will be happy to… ah, wait, Tss’kish is it you rustling towards me?
It has been many seasons. Yet the count escapes me.
Then I shall help you keep track: I’ve added another hundred… perhaps two hundred growth rings since last your roots touched this soil.
And have passing wanderers treated you kindly, old friend?
Bzzz! If you two speak any slower, I’ll drift into hibernation right here on the moss!
Forgive us, little buzzling. Old trees do not trade words swiftly. Tell me — what draws you back? Are you gathering lost knowledge once more?
That is my purpose. But I sense great change here — the land is swollen with memories I cannot reach. A secret lies buried beneath these roots.

Much has changed indeed. Not long past, a powerful mage claimed this region. His arts seeped into the soil — not entirely wicked, yet deadly to any creature bound to life’s flow. We keep our roots away from it where we can.
If secrets you seek, they are his. And you will find the path neither gentle nor simple.
Then this is where wanderers such as we take root. No knowledge shall remain hidden where we stand.

In that case, your next step lies in a lost library, buried in shade and silence. There he worked his craft. It is guarded still, but you break through, the knowledge within will reveal itself. Carry what you find to one who can read the ink patterns.
The druids will help. My gratitude, ancient friend.
Brrr! A hall of dead trees, smeared with colors and wrapped in dead animals…
I must admit… I am humbled. For all his grim arts, this scholar’s mind is vast. These shelves groan beneath the weight of written seasons — scrolls, tomes, memories pressed into ink.
Yay…? Does this mean we’re finished here? Can we go back to the Murmurwoods now? I miss my mushroom dearly.
Not yet, little one. The journey brought us to knowledge… but now comes the slow, patient work — the learning.
[Knowledge increased by 6. You gained the Music Sheet artifact!]
Bandit
Ascension to the Throne
Power is not given, it is taken. Guide Lord Edgar as he dismantles his rivals and seizes control by any means necessary.
Gain power by any means
Lord Edgar must survive
The Inquisition Envoy
Capture the Inquisition Envoy with the Oona’s help
Discover the Envoy’s fate
Escort Anastasia to Oona
Old Lord Mandall has grown noticeably feeble. He can no longer manage his own domain.
No wonder it was so easy for me to seize this vantage point. He’s not even trying to resist. Pitiful old man.
All the better for me. I need to survey the area, scout his lands, and expose his weaknesses.
I will claim power by any means necessary — no matter the cost.
A soft voice drifts from the dense forest. Though quiet, it echoes clearly in your mind, as if the speaker stands right beside you.
I see it… the darkness and envy festering in your heart. You yearn to topple old Mandall and claim his throne.
Cease these games. If you have something to say — say it.
I offer aid. The Lord is old, but his grip is still tight — tighter than yours. I have the means to tip the scales. A poison, to be precise.
You. Are. Wasting. My. Time. Name your price, crone.
Such impatience! Very well. A strange sickness spreads through Mandall’s capital. An envoy of the Inquisition arrives soon to aid him.
This envoy, Anastasia, is no mere priestess. Her soul bears the mark of Arina. I, let us say, have a… particular interest in reading souls. I wish to examine her up close.
She will not come to me willingly, of course. You could, for example, capture her. The precise means make little difference to me.
Bring Anastasia to me, and the poison is yours.
Handing over a sanctimonious girl to a witch? Child’s play.
You freed the girl?! And you still dare to show your face here? Then suffer for it!
A wave of dark energy surges from the forest, slamming into you. Your insides twist in a sudden, agonizing spasm.
What was that?! What did you do to me, witch?!
Only silence answers. Oona is gone.
Well? Do you bring the envoy to me now?
Not yet. Do not presume to order me, old woman.
I grow eager. I want to see how Naira’s power interacts with Arina’s mark upon the girl’s soul.
I’ve brought the envoy. My obligation is fulfilled — now deliver your end of the bargain.
Heh‑heh‑heh! Wonderful, wonderful! What an intriguing soul… Such an intricate pattern. Such emotions! Arina did quite a number on this one.
Take this vial. The poison within is potent enough to taint any crops or water supply you choose. I trust you will find a fitting use for it.
Oona’s voice echoes from the shadows of the bushes.
Word for you: the Inquisition envoy has just entered these lands. Seek her in the west.
Oona’s voice vanishes with a strange echo.
Dozens of armed men spill from their hiding spots onto the road. An ambush.
Well, look what we’ve caught. All dressed up, sparkling with trinkets.
Mind your tongue, rabble. Get out of my path.
Whoa, hold on! Aren’t you that noble brat from the east — the one who scared off all the Lord’s guards? We thank you for that, truly. Without you they’d have been hanging folk like us by the dozen.
But since you’re such a generous noble… you must have something to share. Get him!
The stupidity here is astounding.
Who are you? I was not informed of any high‑ranking officials in these lands b…b‑esides Lord Mandall.
In the name of the Inquisition — identify yourself!
[Capture the Inquisition envoy]
[Walk past without introducing yourself]
Her small escort is completely unprepared for your sudden attack. The fight ends in moments.
Locked in a cage, Anastasia now travels with the convoy behind your army. You never introduced yourself.
My affairs here do not concern the Inquisition.
In the defeated bandit camp, you find Anastasia — the envoy Oona wished you to abduct — locked in a rusted cage.
B…b‑y the Chalice! Help has arrived!
Please, free me b… b… free me b‑before the rest of those scum return!
Yes, sure. Help has arrived. Of course. [Capture the envoy without freeing her]
You’re safe now. The danger is past. You’re free. [Free and release the envoy]
Without unlocking the cage, you order it — Anastasia inside — to be loaded onto one of the supply wagons. She gives you a few disappointed, horrified glances.
Thank you for saving me, stranger. I am Anastasia, envoy of the Inquisition. And you are…?
Lord Edgar. I’m here to… to bring order to these lands.
Then we share a goal! I’ve been sent to c… c… to fight the mysterious illness. There is an artifact in the northeast that would help me greatly.
But first, I must regain my strength. If you need me, or if you find the Truthmaker yourself — look for my camp in the west.
Hmm. She intends to aid Mandall’s people. If I assist her, perhaps I’ll gain leverage over the old man.
Greetings, Lord Edgar! I am always glad to see my rescuer.
How fares the camp? No further attacks from bandits?
Thankfully, none. I’m just g… g‑athering my strength before setting out to find the Truthmaker. I intend to depart soon.
[Give her the Truthmaker artifact]
This is the Truthmaker! You truly found it! This is invaluable — thank you, Lord Edgar!
I shall leave for the city immediately and report your selfless service to Lord Mandall.
It is good to know such selfless p… p‑eople exist. Thank you — and until we meet again!
Until next time, envoy. Until next time.
This village supplies the city garrison with provisions. If something were to happen to their stores, the city’s defense would crumble.
Hmm. This well seems to feed directly into Mandall’s castle. The old man won’t enjoy the water losing its… purity.
I only possess one vial of poison. I must choose carefully.
[Poison the garrison’s food supply]
[Poison the Lord’s water source]
[Return later]
[Food poisoned. The garrison is weakened]
[Water poisoned. Lord Mandall is weakened]
I need to think this through and weigh the consequences.
The Poison is already used up. There’s nothing more to do here.
Halt! You may not trespass upon Lord Mandall’s lands while bearing arms!
You again? Is this some sort of sick jest?
You and your soldiers — leave these grounds now. Otherwise, I will remove you by force.
[Attack]
[Leave]
Enough yapping, Mandall’s dog. Move aside.
No. I swore not to yield ground to monsters. Even those wearing human appearance.
How utterly dramatic. And what a poor taste.
Charge!
Not yet. We fall back — for now.
Retreating with the pitiful remnants of her force, the knight shouted her final words across the field.
You will not defeat Lord Mandall, wretch! You stand no chance in an honest fight!
Whatever. We’ll see.
So cough you are the one sowing chaos in my lands. Lord Edgar. I should have known — my sentries from the east spoke of a “ghastly pale man.”
Why are you doing this? You would inherit your father’s lands regardless. You cough bring suffering for the sake of suffering.
Very well. If fate demands it, I will fight. But know this — even cough victory will not bring you peace.
[Attack]
Wait, Lord Mandall. I believe there has been a misunderstanding. We can negotiate.
Talking seems hard for you, old man. Stop wasting breath. I am not looking for peace.
Yes, my arrival was… messy. But I do not intend harm.
Your lands are in disarray, and I genuinely wish to help restore order.
Nonsense. You have raided my lands, crushed my patrols, and have the cough gall to deny your motives?
Thank the Chalice I made it… Lord Mandall! Lord Edgar is not lying. He truly desires order in these lands.
It was he who retrieved the artifact that saved your p… p… saved your p‑eople from the plague. And he demanded nothing in return!
Unexpected. I am… unsettled. What do you truly want, Edgar? Why come with an army to my gates?

You are wise and experienced. But wisdom requires the reinforcement of cold calculation to keep the domain in order. That is what I offer. I am here to become your advisor and help you restore your authority.
Lord Mandall does not raise his visor, as in his custom. But his fingers tap the stone parapet — a nervous, wavering rhythm.
Talking is easy, but doing the real work cough is another matter. Still, Anastasia says you saved the Inquisition’s people, and honor obliges me to reward you somehow.
So, you want to prove you are someone I can rely on? Fine. You will cough oversee the construction of a temple here. If you manage it properly, consider the advisor’s seat as good as yours.
A temple it is. I won’t disappoint you or the Inquisition.
Thus, I, due to the Inquisitorial recommendation, appoint Lord Edgar as the Sun Temple construction Overseer. May the Chalice guide him.
While the soldiers celebrate avoided bloodshed, no one notices Mandall withdrawing along the wall. The old man stares out into the distance, his fingers drumming the hilt of his sword.

You find Lord Mandall later, seated upon his throne. He has been gravely wounded by a crossbow bolt — his armor pierced, blood seeping through. He holds the wound, breathing heavily. His visor remains lowered.
Was it worth it? All this blood… all this havoc… violent cough for a patch of land?
I do not care how much blood is spilled. Only that it isn’t mine.
[Finish him]
The sword slides between the armor plates and the old lord chokes on one final, desperate fit of coughing.
At last. Done with that feeble, wilted relic.
Power belongs to the strong. And I have proved my strength.
Victory has many forms. Now I stand at Mandall’s court — beside him, behind him. And he already knows he has lost.
Song of the Murmurwoods
Find the lines of the ancient song to summon the aid of the Murmurwoods and defeat the blazing army of Grove traitor.
Grove Protectors
Summon the Four Elements
As herbs and flowers…
The trees awake…
Thus faeries weave…
The ice‑cold river…
Lake shores…
The rain washes down…
Fierce gusts…
Zephyrs whisper…
Winds stir…
…the fire glows.
…the flames expose.
…amid the enemy’s bones.
Gingertail and Aunt Daliar must survive
Defeat Tavi
These infernal creatures keep attacking us, and still I cannot track down their leader. She slips through every shadow, gathering power unseen.
Indeed. It’s no wonder she excels at this. I always knew the day would come when that traitor, Taviola, returned. Her grudge against the Murmurwoods runs far too deep.
Return? Auntie… Are you saying this creature was once one of our own?
Yes, child. Among us, there are those for whom the Murmurwoods’ bounty is never enough, no matter how freely it is given. Taviola was such a soul, and for her greed she was cast out.
Yet while she lived among us, none could hide as well as she could. That, it seems, has not changed.
Many things make sense now. But time is running out. If I don’t find her soon, she’ll grow too powerful to stop — and I have no idea left to try! What should I do, Auntie?
The Murmurwoods themselves shall guide thee. The Mycelium holds the knowledge of what lurks beneath leaf and shadow. It will reveal the hidden — if one knows how to ask.
Thy voice will be the key, Gingertail. Seek the shards of the ancient Song of the Four Elements, and weave them together once more. Then sing — sing to the Mycelium.
I cannot walk beside thee. I must remain here, keeping watch on these abominations and protecting the city. Good luck on thy path, child.
I will face whatever awaits us, I promise! The Mycelium will hear my song!
A rasping voice tears through the thicket, rising from nowhere and everywhere at once. Its echoes scatter like frightened birds.
How dare you wield this magic against me?! I — who was robbed once already by this deceitful forest?!
Fortunately, I’ve found true allies, brothers and sisters. And soon enough, you will meet one of them… if that is what you so desperately seek.
Another of their generals approaches. Ready yourselves — we must defend the city.
Enough! Once again, your precious forest has ruined my plans. Very well, I will end this myself, here and now, once and for all.
The Song has deepened thy bonds with the Mycelium, and the demonic spawns can no longer hide in the shadow of its ancient trees. Thy voice has worked a miracle, child.
Yet this is not the end. The final trial awaits. We must lay this wayward traitor to rest — before she calls in reinforcements.
We’ll handle it, Auntie. Honestly, after piecing together the ancient song of the Murmurwoods, this hardly feels like a challenge.
I feel a tremor in the forest’s heart… their horrific army is preparing to strike.
The fauns bring dire news — the burning host swells in number. Their attack draws near, and their army is larger now.
Our sentries see strange flames above the treetops. The dark forces grow stronger. Their army is even bigger than before.
The heat of the infernal fire creeps through the Murmurwood chill. Their power is greater than we have ever witnessed. The attack is inevitable, and it’s about to begin.
You wanted this meeting so desperately you even resorted to singing your little elven songs — all to coax the forest revealing me.
Very well. You have what you sought. Now you will witness the true might. Power unbound by your Mycelium or any other foolish restraint.
So be it.
Foolish fae… One day we shall return…
And the woods will stand ready.

Roots of the nearest trees come to life, weaving themselves into a deliberate pattern across the soil. As the shape takes form, you realize it isn’t random at all — it’s a piece of writing, a new line of the Song.
As herbs and flowers rise and bloom,
The trees awake from ancient sloom,
Thus faeries weave their rings of shroom,
The babbling of the nearby stream shifts, its cadence suddenly unfamiliar. Amid the rush of water, quiet words emerge, carrying the soft whisper of a new line of the Song.
The ice‑cold river flows.
Lake shores still hide the lurking foes.
The rain washes down our woes.
A great gust strikes you from behind, sharp enough that you have to grab a tree branch to stay upright. A maple leaf catches on the branch and begins to glow, revealing a line of the Song.
Fierce gusts shall scatter all the gloom,
Zephyrs whisper warnings of the doom,
So do winds stir above the forgotten tomb,

A flame sparks to life unbidden upon the bare stone. You stare at it for a few moments, entranced by its dance. When you come back to your senses, a new line of the Song echoes through your mind — and the flame has already vanished.
In places where the fire glows.
And what darkness conceals, the flames expose.
And phoenix rises amid the enemy’s bones.
The time has come to sing the Song of The Four Elements. I only hope I’ve memorized it well.
[Sing]
[Wait]
[Begin with the Earth line]
As herbs and flowers rise and bloom,
The trees awake from ancient sloom,
Thus faeries weave their rings of shroom,
[Continue with the Water line]
The ice‑cold river flows.
Lake shores still hide the lurking foes.
The rain washes down our woes.
[Add the Air line]
Fierce gusts shall scatter all the gloom,
Zephyrs whisper warnings of the doom,
Winds stir above the forgotten tomb,
[Finish with the Fire line]
In places where the fire glows.
And what darkness conceals, the flames expose.
And phoenix rises amid the enemy’s bones.
The echo of the Song’s final notes faded into the thick foliage. For a moment, absolute silence settled over the Murmurwoods.
Then, without warning, a powerful wind tore through the branches, forcing them apart. The ground began to shift, pushing aside bushes and boulders and even altering the paths of rivers.

The sky darkened beneath sudden clouds, and a wall of heavy rain crashed down upon the forest. Dozens of lightning bolts split the air above the treetops, each flash revealing a silhouette in the storm — Taviola, rising atop one of the hills.
Uh… maybe I messed up one of the lines. What if I try it a different way…
Thou did it, child! The forest has awakened and revealed the traitor to us.
Disturbed Pixie
Pixie
All Cats go to Heaven
Mreowa seeks a place known as the “Cat Paradise.” The path leads through a mysterious forest shrouded in fog.
Clear the fog blocking your path
Reach the Cat Paradise
Whew, I finally made it! This place looks exactly as the old writings descrrribed — the road to the “cat parrradise.”
I already feel strrrange here, being so close to such a place. Almost… exalted.
I’m not exactly a cat, of course — but surely an Almandine will be welcome there as well. Assuming the place even exists.
I only need to figure out how to cross this forrrest. The “parrradise” should lie somewhere beyond the trees.
Am I alive? I clearly rrremember losing… and then…
I once heard the phrrrase “cats have nine lives.” Perhaps being so close to the paradise grrants me another chance?
It still makes no sense. I feel no pain — as if the battle never happened.
It’s unsettling. My furrr is standing on end with all these mysteries.
The forest is falling apart… it’s falling apart… it’s falling apart…
Fog… more fog… it comes from there… from the deep… the fog…
Hey, are you alright? Trrry to steady yourself. What is happening? What is this fog?
Foooog… the fog…
I won’t get anything out of her like this. I need to rrreach her somehow.
You are safe. I can prrrotect you from the fog. Everything is fine, little crrreature.
I need your help to stop the fog. Only you can explain it and help me save the forrrest.
Safe?.. Safe. I am safe.
Her eyes clear. She shakes her head as if waking from a trance, then looks at you with surprise.
Thank you. I… don’t know what came over me. My thoughts feel tangled, it is so hard to think straight.
Help? We need help! The fog!… The fooog!.. Wait, help?.. Defeat the fog?
To defeat the fog… yes, I can help with that. I can. Thank you, I still don’t know what came over me. My thoughts keep slipping away.
I am glad I could help you. Tell me calmly: what is this fog, and where does it come from? How do we get rrrid of it?
I don’t know how it appeared. I don’t even remember how I ended up here myself. It must be the shock. This fog… it does something to the mind.
I tried to dispel it with this, but I guess I wasn’t strong enough.
She hands you a crystal shard. Your Almandine nature senses it immediately: the crystal is pulsing with pure magical power, feeling strangely familiar.
If you focus its power at the source — where the mist is thickest — you can burn it out.
Perrrfect. I know how to handle crrrystals.
I have already weakened this patch of fog. You should be able to burn it away right here.
You take the crystal and reach for the invisible threads of energy in the air. It is surprisingly easy — as if the crystal has been waiting for your touch.
The fog ignites and vanishes instantly, like dry cloth thrown into a fire. You tuck the crystal into your bag, wondering why the magic felt so effortless.
Wonderrrful. Thank you for the help, little one. Do you know how far it is from here to the “cat parrradise”?
“Cat Paradise”? I suppose I might have known once, but as I said, my memory is a void. I’m not even sure where “here” is…
I need to recover. Thank you for saving me.
No prrroblem at all. Good luck, little creature.
The way ahead is blocked by that same magical fog.
I won’t be able to get thrrrough it. We must find a way to clear it.
That same magical fog blocks the path ahead.
I cannot path through it. I must find the place where the fog forms and use the crrrystal.
[Clear this patch of fog]
[Return later]
You unravel the nearest knot of energy and channel it through the crystal. A beam of light strikes the heart of the mist, searing it away in seconds.
For a fleeting moment, a faint whisper echoes inside your head, then vanishes.
Time to move forrrward. My goal lies deeper in the forrrest.
You find another source of the fog. Another ideal place to drive back the gloom with the crystal’s magic.
[Dispel the fog]
[Return later]
The crystal pulses: the fog parts as if torn by invisible claws. Thick scraps of mist scatter and fade.
As the last bit dissolves, another foreign voice echoes clearly in your mind.
How… many… already…
What? Who is that? Show yourself!

So cold… the frost bites, it stings worse than the little ones. The little ones, they freeze — and that’s not right, no. Nowhere to hide in the white, nowhere to burrow. We hate it here. We need the soot of the city… the rot of the fields…
